name: Stargate — Abilene (Lancium Clean Campus)
notes: Flagship Stargate site. First 2 buildings (~0.3 GW) energized Sep 2025; ~4 of 8 buildings live by 2026; building toward ~1.2 GW. ~450k GB200 GPUs reported (DCD). Bridge power from on-site gas while ERCOT interconnect builds out.

name: Stargate — Shackelford County, TX
notes: One of five Stargate sites announced Sep 2025; developed with Vantage.

name: Stargate — Milam County, TX
notes: Stargate site developed with SB Energy (SoftBank).

name: Stargate — Doña Ana County, NM
notes: Southern NM site near El Paso metro; WECC rather than ERCOT.

name: Stargate — Lordstown, OH
notes: PJM site — exposed to PJM capacity-price pressure and interconnection queue.

name: Stargate — Port Washington, WI

sources.detail: MISO (We Energies); new gas generation planned

notes: The previously-unnamed 'Midwest' Stargate site; developed by Oracle + Vantage.

name: Stargate — Saline Township, MI
notes: Reported Stargate expansion site with DTE; details still firming up.

name: Meta Hyperion — Richland Parish, LA

sources.detail: Entergy 3x new combined-cycle gas plants (~2.26 GW) for Meta

notes: Meta's largest campus (~5 GW). Entergy building 3 new CCGTs (~2.26 GW) plus a solar fleet; first phase ~2026.

name: Meta Prometheus — New Albany, OH
notes: Meta's first ~1 GW 'titan cluster', targeted online 2026; among the first gigawatt-scale AI campuses.

name: Meta — El Paso, TX
notes: ~1 GW Meta campus announced for El Paso; WECC market.

name: Meta — Eagle Mountain, UT
notes: Long-running Meta campus being expanded for AI.

name: Meta — Temple, TX
notes: Smaller Meta campus (~152 MW, ~$800M), redesigned for AI, completing 2026 — corrected down from an earlier ~0.9 GW overestimate that conflated it with a separate gigawatt West-Texas/El Paso site.

name: Meta — Montgomery, AL
notes: Southeast Meta campus in Southern Company territory.

name: Meta — Sandston (Henrico), VA
notes: Established Meta campus in PJM/Dominion territory, expanding for AI.

name: Microsoft Fairwater — Mount Pleasant, WI
notes: Microsoft's 'Fairwater' closed-loop liquid-cooled design; first facility online early 2026. Part of a ~2 GW multi-site AI WAN.

name: Microsoft Fairwater 2 — Atlanta, GA
notes: Second Fairwater site, linked to WI via dedicated-fiber AI WAN.

name: Microsoft — San Antonio, TX
notes: Microsoft/OpenAI San Antonio cluster in ERCOT.

name: Microsoft — Goodyear / Phoenix, AZ
notes: Phoenix-metro Azure region expanding into AI capacity.

name: Microsoft — West Des Moines, IA
notes: Major Azure/OpenAI training region; historically where large GPT models were trained.

name: Microsoft — Quincy, WA
notes: Hydro-powered legacy Microsoft campus; low-carbon baseload advantage.

name: AWS Project Rainier — New Carlisle, IN

sources.detail: MISO (I&M / AEP); ~2.2 GW at full 30-building build

notes: Built for Anthropic. ~500k Trainium2 live; full site ~30 buildings, up to ~2.2 GW, targeting 1M+ Trainium2 chips. Custom-ASIC (Trainium), not Nvidia.

name: AWS — Madison County, MS
notes: Large AWS Mississippi investment (~$16B); Trainium + Nvidia mix.

name: AWS — Northern Virginia (Data Center Alley)
notes: Representative marker for AWS's multi-GW Loudoun County footprint — 'Data Center Alley', the largest concentration of capacity in the world. MW shown is an order-of-magnitude estimate.

name: AWS — Salem Township, PA (Susquehanna)

sources.detail: Talen Susquehanna nuclear PPA (1,920 MW to AWS through 2042)

notes: Anchored to Talen's Susquehanna nuclear plant; FERC rejected the behind-the-meter version, so it now runs through a front-of-meter PPA (1,920 MW).

name: AWS — New Albany / Central Ohio
notes: AWS's large Central Ohio (US-East-2) cluster; ~$23B announced Ohio expansion.

name: Google — Council Bluffs, IA
notes: Major Google TPU region; Anthropic runs Claude on Google TPUs (expanded to 1M+ TPUs in 2025-26).

name: Google — The Dalles, OR
notes: Google's original owned data center, hydro-powered.

name: Google — New Albany, OH
notes: Google Central Ohio campus expansion.

name: Google — Armstrong County, TX (Panhandle)
notes: Texas Panhandle is SPP, not ERCOT; abundant wind.

name: Google — Mesa, AZ
notes: Phoenix-metro Google campus.

name: Google — Berkeley County, SC
notes: Southeast Google campus.

name: xAI Colossus 1 — Memphis, TN

sources.detail: On-site mobile gas turbines (Solaris/VoltaGrid) + permitted gas plant

notes: Fastest-built large cluster (~122 days to first training). Heavy on-site gas drew air-permit scrutiny in Memphis. ~230k GPUs.

name: xAI Colossus 2 — Memphis, TN
notes: Second Memphis-area Colossus campus targeting ~1 GW+ on a ~12-month timeline. Its ~19 on-site gas turbines are sited just across the state line in Southaven, MS.

name: Anthropic / Fluidstack — Texas
notes: Part of Anthropic's ~$50B Fluidstack-built infrastructure plan (TX + NY). Exact site/coordinates approximate.

name: Anthropic / Fluidstack — New York
notes: Second leg of the Anthropic/Fluidstack plan; upstate NY has spare nuclear/hydro baseload. Coordinates approximate.

name: CoreWeave / Core Scientific — Denton, TX

sources.detail: ERCOT (former crypto-mining site converted to AI)

notes: Neocloud conversion of Core Scientific crypto sites to GPU capacity (CoreWeave acquired Core Scientific).

name: Poolside Project Horizon — West Texas
notes: ~2 GW West Texas campus using stranded Permian gas; CoreWeave-backed.

name: Switch Citadel — Tahoe Reno, NV
notes: Large Tahoe-Reno colo campus; multi-tenant, increasingly AI.

name: Homer City Energy Campus — PA

sources.detail: Retired coal plant -> ~4.5 GW on-site gas (7x GE Vernova turbines) for behind-the-meter data center campus

notes: Former largest coal plant in PA being redeveloped into one of the largest gas-powered behind-the-meter AI campuses; ~4.5 GW on-site generation.

name: QTS — Fayetteville, GA

sources.detail: Georgia Power (SERC) — a major driver of GA load growth

notes: QTS (Blackstone-owned) wholesale campus leased to hyperscalers; Atlanta is a top US growth market.

name: QTS — Phoenix, AZ
notes: Phoenix is one of the largest and fastest-growing US data-center metros.

name: Crusoe — Cheyenne, WY
notes: Crusoe (the Stargate Abilene builder) developing a separate Wyoming gas-powered campus.

name: AWS — Umatilla / Morrow County, OR
notes: AWS's large eastern-Oregon (US-West-2) cluster.

name: Google — Pryor (MidAmerica), OK
notes: Google Oklahoma campus in SPP; wind-heavy.
